Operating Files  
Human Approval  
Rights Review  
Creation Rights Readiness  
Protected Records  
Asset Reuse  
Decision Rights

## **Rule**

Every resource article should point to a practical next step.

Most common CTA:

Book a C/Skills Review

---

# **9\. Form Routing Structure**

## **Form Routes**

Book review form routes to:

/thank-you/review

Contact form routes to:

/thank-you/contact

Partnership form routes to:

/thank-you/partnership

Briefing form routes to:

/thank-you/briefing

Newsletter form routes to:

/thank-you/newsletter

## **Form Build Rule**

No public file upload at launch.

Each form must include the confidentiality warning.

Each form must have clear labels and accessible error states.

---

# **10\. SEO Structure**

## **Headline**

SEO should be generated from content data.

## **SEO Fields**

title  
description  
canonical URL  
open graph title  
open graph description  
open graph image if used  
robots setting

## **Robots Rules**

Index launch pages.

Noindex thank-you pages.

Noindex internal planning pages.

Noindex staged pages until approved.

Noindex draft legal pages before counsel review.

---

# **11\. Navigation Structure**

## **Header Links**

What We Do  
Engagements  
Who We Help  
Creation Rights Readiness  
About  
Book a C/Skills Review

## **Engagement Dropdown**

C/Skills Review  
Operating Model Design  
Agent Operating Blueprint  
Governance Blueprint  
Pilot Transformation  
Implementation Office

## **Footer Columns**

Creation Skills  
Engagements  
Ecosystem  
Resources  
Legal

## **Rule**

Do not expose all phase two, phase three, or internal pages in the top navigation.

---

# **12\. Design Token Structure**

## **Colors**

colors \= {  
  lime: "...",  
  charcoal: "...",  
  nearBlack: "...",  
  warmWhite: "...",  
  softNeutral: "...",  
  mutedGray: "...",  
  border: "..."  
}

## **Typography**

type \= {  
  hero: "...",  
  h1: "...",  
  h2: "...",  
  h3: "...",  
  body: "...",  
  small: "...",  
  eyebrow: "..."  
}

## **Spacing**

spacing \= {  
  section: "...",  
  sectionSmall: "...",  
  container: "...",  
  cardGap: "...",  
  hero: "..."  
}

## **Rule**

Keep visual tokens centralized so the site can evolve without redesigning every page.

---

# **13\. Build Order**

## **Step 1**

Set up global layout, tokens, typography, and base styles.

## **Step 2**

Build Header, Footer, Container, Section, Button, Card, and FinalCTA.

## **Step 3**

Build core templates:

Core marketing page  
Engagement page  
Utility page  
Thank-you page  
Resource article page

## **Step 4**

Add launch content data.

## **Step 5**

Build forms and confirmation routes.

## **Step 6**

Add SEO metadata.

## **Step 7**

QA mobile, accessibility, links, forms, and legal placeholders.

## **Step 8**

Stage optional resource pages only if approved.

---

# **14\. Build Do Not**

## **Do Not**

Do not build every master document page into navigation.

Do not expose internal planning pages.

Do not use lorem ipsum.

Do not use fake Gemini artifact text.

Do not add file uploads to public forms.

Do not use generic AI imagery.

Do not confuse Creation Skills with Creation Rights.

Do not confuse Creation Skills with CreativeIP.org.

Do not publish legal pages without review.

Do not create one-off page designs for every route.

---

# **15\. Build QA Checklist**

## **Checklist**

Routes match the route map.  
Header links work.  
Footer links work.  
Dynamic engagement pages work.  
Forms route to correct thank-you pages.  
SEO metadata appears on every public page.  
Thank-you pages are noindex.  
Internal pages are not public.  
No staged pages are indexed.  
Mobile menu works.  
Forms are accessible.  
Confidentiality warning appears near forms.  
NILP® mark is correct.  
C/Skills. is correct.  
A Creation Rights Division is correct.  
Core line appears where appropriate.  
Site feels premium and creative.

---

# **Final Note**

The build should make the Creation Skills system easy to expand.

The launch site should stay focused.

The codebase should be ready for the full master document later.

# Analytics and Measurement Plan

# **Creation Skills Internal Page: Analytics and Measurement Plan**

## **Page Identity**

**Creation Skills**  
**C/Skills.**  
**A Creation Rights Division**

## **Document Type**

Internal analytics and measurement planning document

## **Public Status**

Not a public page

## **Purpose**

This page defines how Creation Skills should measure website performance, form intent, content engagement, and conversion quality after launch.

The goal is not to over-track visitors.

The goal is to understand whether the site helps serious companies understand Creation Skills, explore the right engagement, and request a C/Skills Review.

Analytics should be privacy-aware, legally reviewed, and aligned with the final Privacy Notice and Cookie Policy.

---

# **1\. Measurement Principle**

## **Headline**

Measure the path to serious enquiry.

## **Body**

Creation Skills is not a high-volume consumer website.

It is a premium consulting and transformation site for serious companies working around media creation, production agents, operating files, approval gates, rights review, protected records, and Creation Rights readiness.

The most important measurement is not raw traffic.

The most important measurement is whether the right visitors understand the offer and take the right next step.

## **Core Measurement Question**

Are the right visitors moving from understanding to enquiry?

---

# **2\. Primary Conversion**

## **Eyebrow**

PRIMARY CONVERSION

## **Headline**

The main conversion is a C/Skills Review request.

## **Primary Goal**

A qualified visitor submits the Book a C/Skills Review form.

## **Primary Conversion Route**

/book-review  
/thank-you/review

## **Primary Conversion Event**

book\_review\_form\_submit

## **Supporting CTA Events**

cta\_book\_review\_click  
book\_review\_form\_view  
book\_review\_form\_start  
book\_review\_form\_submit

## **What This Measures**

Whether the site is converting serious visitors into focused review requests around one production path.

---

# **3\. Secondary Conversions**

## **Eyebrow**

SECONDARY CONVERSIONS

## **Headline**

Secondary conversions support routing and relationship building.

## **Secondary Goals**

### **Contact Form Submission**

A visitor submits a general enquiry.

**Route:** /contact  
**Confirmation:** /thank-you/contact  
**Event:** contact\_form\_submit

### **Partnership Enquiry**

A potential partner submits a partnership enquiry.

**Route:** /ecosystem/partners  
**Confirmation:** /thank-you/partnership  
**Event:** partnership\_form\_submit

### **Briefing Request**

A visitor requests a briefing, panel, or private session.

**Route:** /ecosystem/speaking-and-briefings  
**Confirmation:** /thank-you/briefing  
**Event:** briefing\_form\_submit

### **Newsletter Signup**

A visitor signs up for Creation Skills updates.

**Route:** /resources  
**Confirmation:** /thank-you/newsletter  
**Event:** newsletter\_signup\_submit

---

# **4\. Key Pages to Measure**

## **Eyebrow**

PAGE PERFORMANCE

## **Headline**

Track the pages that explain and convert.

## **Core Pages**

Home  
What We Do  
Engagements  
C/Skills Review  
Agent Operating Blueprint  
Governance Blueprint  
Creation Rights Readiness  
Who We Help  
Book a C/Skills Review  
Contact

## **Why These Matter**

These pages carry the main buyer journey.

They explain:

What Creation Skills is.  
What problem it solves.  
How the engagements work.  
Who it helps.  
How it connects to Creation Rights.  
How to request a C/Skills Review.

---

# **5\. Core Events**

## **Eyebrow**

EVENTS

## **Headline**

Track simple events first.

## **Recommended Launch Events**

### **Navigation Events**

nav\_what\_we\_do\_click  
nav\_engagements\_click  
nav\_who\_we\_help\_click  
nav\_creation\_rights\_readiness\_click  
nav\_about\_click  
nav\_book\_review\_click

### **CTA Events**

cta\_book\_review\_click  
cta\_engagements\_click  
cta\_creation\_rights\_readiness\_click  
cta\_contact\_click  
cta\_resources\_click

### **Form Events**

book\_review\_form\_view  
book\_review\_form\_start  
book\_review\_form\_submit  
contact\_form\_view  
contact\_form\_start  
contact\_form\_submit  
partnership\_form\_submit  
briefing\_form\_submit  
newsletter\_signup\_submit

### **Engagement Events**

engagement\_card\_click  
audience\_card\_click  
resource\_card\_click  
footer\_link\_click

## **Rule**

Do not overbuild analytics at launch.

Start with simple events that help understand intent.

---

# **6\. Conversion Funnel**

## **Eyebrow**

FUNNEL

## **Headline**

The main funnel should be easy to read.

## **Primary Funnel**

### **Step 1**

Visitor lands on Home, What We Do, Engagements, Who We Help, or Creation Rights Readiness.

### **Step 2**

Visitor clicks Book a C/Skills Review.

### **Step 3**

Visitor views the Book a C/Skills Review form.

### **Step 4**

Visitor starts the form.

### **Step 5**

Visitor submits the form.

### **Step 6**

Visitor reaches Review Confirmation page.

## **Funnel Events**

page\_view  
cta\_book\_review\_click  
book\_review\_form\_view  
book\_review\_form\_start  
book\_review\_form\_submit  
thank\_you\_review\_view

---

# **7\. Quality Signals**

## **Eyebrow**

QUALITY

## **Headline**

Quality matters more than volume.

## **Strong Fit Signals**

Visitor views more than one engagement page.

Visitor views Creation Rights Readiness.

Visitor views Agent Operating Blueprint.

Visitor views Governance Blueprint.

Visitor views Who We Help.

Visitor starts or submits Book a C/Skills Review.

Visitor selects a real production path.

Visitor mentions production agents, approvals, rights review, NILP®, reuse, records, or Creation Rights adoption.

## **Weak Fit Signals**

Visitor only asks for generic AI tips.

Visitor only wants a course.

Visitor only wants legal advice.

Visitor only wants a software demo.

Visitor cannot name a production path.

Visitor asks to upload confidential files through public forms.

---

# **8\. Lead Source Tracking**

## **Eyebrow**

LEAD SOURCE

## **Headline**

Capture where serious enquiries come from.

## **Suggested UTM Fields**

utm\_source  
utm\_medium  
utm\_campaign  
utm\_content  
utm\_term

## **Suggested Source Categories**

Direct  
Search  
LinkedIn  
Email  
Partner referral  
Creation Rights referral  
CreativeIP.org referral  
Event or briefing  
Sales outreach  
Press or media  
Other

## **Form Data Fields**

source\_page  
landing\_page  
referrer  
utm\_source  
utm\_medium  
utm\_campaign  
submission\_timestamp

## **Rule**

Source tracking should support business learning without collecting unnecessary sensitive information.

---

# **9\. Content Performance**

## **Eyebrow**

CONTENT

## **Headline**

Measure which ideas create interest.

## **Content Areas to Watch**

Production agents  
Operating files  
Human approval gates  
Rights review  
NILP® checks  
Asset reuse  
Protected records  
Creation Rights readiness  
Legal and rights teams  
Platform and digital asset teams  
Executive sponsors

## **Useful Metrics**

Page views  
CTA clicks  
Scroll depth if enabled  
Related page clicks  
Resource card clicks  
Time on page if available  
Form starts after page visit  
Form submissions after page visit

## **Interpretation**

A page is valuable if it helps the right visitor understand the offer and move closer to enquiry.

A page is not valuable just because it gets traffic.

---

# **10\. Resource Measurement**

## **Eyebrow**

RESOURCES

## **Headline**

Resources should support the sales path.

## **First Resource Pages to Measure**

Why Production Agents Need Owners  
From Loose Prompts to Operating Files  
The Human Approval Gate  
Why Rights Review Belongs Earlier  
Creation Rights Readiness

## **Resource Success Signals**

Visitor clicks from article to Book a C/Skills Review.

Visitor clicks from article to relevant engagement page.

Visitor clicks from article to Creation Rights Readiness.

Visitor subscribes to updates.

Visitor spends enough time to read.

Visitor returns later and submits a review request.

## **Resource Rule**

Resources should not become generic traffic pages.

They should help explain Creation Skills and move qualified visitors toward the right next step.

---

# **11\. Form Analytics**

## **Eyebrow**

FORM ANALYTICS

## **Headline**

Track form completion without collecting sensitive materials.

## **Track**

Form views  
Form starts  
Form submissions  
Field-level errors where useful  
Drop-off before submit  
Confirmation page views

## **Do Not Track Publicly**

Confidential message contents  
Sensitive legal facts  
Private talent details  
Contracts  
Files  
Credentials  
Private links  
Sensitive rights notes

## **Rule**

Analytics should measure form behavior, not expose sensitive content.

---

# **12\. Privacy and Cookie Alignment**

## **Eyebrow**

PRIVACY

## **Headline**

Analytics must match the Privacy Notice and Cookie Policy.

## **Body**

Before launch, confirm which tools are actually being used.

The Privacy Notice and Cookie Policy should reflect:

Analytics tools  
Form tools  
CRM tools  
Scheduling tools  
Hosting tools  
Cookie banner or consent tool  
Marketing or retargeting tools if any

## **Privacy Rules**

Do not add advertising or retargeting tools without updating the Cookie Policy.

Do not collect unnecessary personal information.

Do not track sensitive form message content in analytics.

Do not expose private enquiry data in analytics dashboards.

Do not treat form submissions as public data.

## **Counsel Note**

Final analytics and cookie setup should be reviewed against applicable privacy requirements before publication.

---

# **13\. Dashboard View**

## **Eyebrow**

REPORTING

## **Headline**

Use a simple dashboard for launch.

## **Suggested Dashboard Sections**

### **Traffic Overview**

Sessions  
Users  
Top landing pages  
Top traffic sources

### **Buyer Journey**

CTA clicks  
Engagement page views  
Creation Rights Readiness views  
Book Review page views  
Form starts  
Form submissions

### **Content Interest**

Top viewed pages  
Top clicked engagement cards  
Top clicked audience cards  
Top resource pages

### **Lead Quality**

Qualified review requests  
General enquiries  
Partnership enquiries  
Briefing requests  
Weak-fit enquiries  
Creation Rights readiness enquiries

### **Technical Health**

404 hits  
Form errors  
Slow pages  
Mobile issues  
Broken links

---

# **14\. Review Cadence**

## **Eyebrow**

CADENCE

## **Headline**

Review analytics in small cycles.

## **Launch Review Schedule**

### **Week 1**

Check form submissions, broken links, 404s, and top landing pages.

### **Week 2**

Review CTA clicks, form starts, and form drop-off.

### **Month 1**

Review page performance, lead quality, and messaging clarity.

### **Month 2**

Decide whether to publish resources or phase two concept pages.

### **Month 3**

Review which audience pages should be prioritized.

## **Rule**

Use analytics to improve clarity, not to chase random traffic.

---

# **15\. Decision Rules**

## **Eyebrow**

DECISIONS

## **Headline**

Use measurement to decide what to improve next.

## **If Visitors View Pages but Do Not Click CTAs**

Improve CTA placement, page clarity, or final CTA language.

## **If Visitors Click Book Review but Do Not Submit**

Improve form length, form clarity, trust language, or confidentiality note.

## **If Many Visitors Ask Generic AI Questions**

Clarify that Creation Skills is not generic AI consulting.

## **If Many Visitors Ask for Legal Advice**

Strengthen legal boundary and rights review process language.

## **If Many Visitors Ask for Software Demo**

Clarify Creation Skills vs Creation Rights platform.

## **If Creation Rights Readiness Performs Well**

Consider promoting it more in the homepage and footer.

## **If Agent Operating Blueprint Performs Well**

Consider publishing Company-Owned Production Agents and Operating Files pages sooner.

---

# **16\. Analytics QA Checklist**

## **Checklist**

Analytics tool selected.  
Privacy Notice updated to match tool.  
Cookie Policy updated to match tool.  
Cookie consent approach confirmed if needed.  
Form events defined.  
CTA events defined.  
Thank-you pages noindexed.  
Thank-you page views tracked.  
Internal pages excluded.  
Staged pages excluded.  
Test submissions excluded where possible.  
No confidential message content tracked in analytics.  
Dashboard owner assigned.  
Review cadence agreed.

---

# **Final Note**

Measure what matters.

Creation Skills should not optimize for empty traffic.

It should optimize for the right companies understanding the offer and requesting a focused C/Skills Review.

# Legal Review Tracker and Counsel Handoff

# **Creation Skills Internal Page: Legal Review Tracker and Counsel Handoff**

## **Page Identity**

**Creation Skills**  
**C/Skills.**  
**A Creation Rights Division**

## **Document Type**

Internal legal review planning document

## **Public Status**

Not a public page

## **Purpose**

This page tracks the Creation Skills pages, language, forms, notices, and claims that should be reviewed by counsel before publication.

Creation Skills is a consulting and transformation division of Creation Rights. It works around media creation processes, production agents, operating files, human approval gates, rights review, NILP® checks, protected records, metadata, asset reuse, and Creation Rights readiness.

Because some pages reference rights, NILP®, consent, talent usage, protected records, privacy, cookies, and legal boundaries, legal review should happen before launch where required.

---

# **1\. Legal Review Principle**

## **Headline**

Process design language should stay separate from legal advice.

## **Body**

Creation Skills can describe process design, review points, approval gates, operating files, escalation paths, protected record models, and Creation Rights readiness.

Creation Skills should not present itself as legal counsel.

Legal decisions remain with the client and its advisors.

## **Core Legal Boundary**

Creation Skills supports process design. It does not replace legal counsel. Legal decisions remain with the client and its advisors.

## **Rule**

Every rights-sensitive page should include clear boundary language.

---

# **2\. Pages Requiring Legal Review**

## **Eyebrow**

LEGAL REVIEW REQUIRED

## **Headline**

These pages should not publish as final without counsel review.

## **Pages**

### **Privacy Notice**

Review for data collection, form submissions, analytics, cookies, CRM, retention, privacy rights, service providers, international processing, and contact details.

### **Terms of Use**

Review for website use, no client relationship, no legal advice, content ownership, acceptable use, liability, governing law, and venue.

### **Cookie Policy**

Review for actual cookie tools, analytics tools, consent approach, marketing cookies, third-party providers, and browser privacy signals.

### **Legal Notice / Company Information**

Review for entity name, registered address, jurisdiction, company number, trademark notices, copyright line, legal contact, and governing law placeholders.

### **Confidentiality and Client Materials**

Review for sensitive-material instructions, confidentiality language, client process language, and limits on public form submissions.

### **Rights Review and NILP® Checks**

Review for rights, consent, NILP®, talent usage, paid media, reuse, legal boundary language, and avoidance of clearance claims.

### **Protected Records and Metadata**

Review for protected record language, metadata, audit references, consent, talent usage, platform handoff, and privacy-sensitive data references.

### **Asset Reuse and Usage Controls**

Review for usage scope, paid media, reuse, talent usage, NILP®, consent, territory, channel, duration, and legal boundary language.

### **Responsible Agent Use**

Review for agent limits, blocked actions, rights flagging, no autonomous approval, no legal determinations, and human approval language.

### **Legal, Rights, and Risk Teams**

Review for legal-team positioning, legal boundary, rights review, product claims, talent usage, and process design claims.

### **Why Rights Review Belongs Earlier**

Review for legal-sensitive article language, rights review, NILP®, consent, paid media, reuse, and no-legal-advice boundary.

---

# **3\. Pages That Need Content Review, Not Full Legal Review**

## **Eyebrow**

CONTENT REVIEW

## **Headline**

These pages should be checked for accuracy and boundaries.

## **Pages**

Home  
What We Do  
Engagements  
C/Skills Review  
Operating Model Design  
Agent Operating Blueprint  
Governance Blueprint  
Pilot Transformation  
Implementation Office  
Who We Help  
Creation Rights Readiness  
About  
Book a C/Skills Review  
Contact  
Resources Index  
The Human Approval Gate  
Creation Rights Readiness Resource Article

## **Review Focus**

Creation Skills is consulting.  
Creation Rights is platform.  
CreativeIP.org is education and standards.  
No legal advice claims.  
No compliance guarantee.  
No rights clearance claim.  
No automatic approval claim.  
No overpromising around agents.  
No confidentiality promise beyond approved process.

---

# **4\. Trademark and Brand Review**

## **Eyebrow**

TRADEMARKS

## **Headline**

Confirm brand and mark language before launch.

## **Items to Confirm**

Creation Skills brand ownership  
C/Skills. brand mark usage  
Creation Rights ownership and usage  
NILP® trademark ownership  
NILP® registered mark usage  
CreativeIP.org reference language  
Copyright line  
Footer trademark notice  
Any required disclaimer language

## **Draft Footer Legal Line**

© \[Year\] Creation Rights. Creation Skills is a Creation Rights division. NILP® is a registered mark.

## **Counsel Note**

Confirm whether the footer should name the legal entity, brand owner, or operating company.

---

# **5\. Entity and Company Information**

## **Eyebrow**

COMPANY DETAILS

## **Headline**

Confirm company details before publishing legal pages.

## **Items to Confirm**

Legal entity name  
Trading name if applicable  
Registered address  
Company number if applicable  
Jurisdiction  
Governing law  
Venue  
Legal contact email  
Privacy contact email  
Accessibility contact email  
General contact email  
Parent or affiliated entity language  
Division language

## **Placeholder Fields**

Registered Entity: \[Insert legal entity name\]  
Registered Address: \[Insert address\]  
Company Number: \[Insert if applicable\]  
Jurisdiction: \[Insert jurisdiction\]  
Governing Law: \[Insert governing law\]  
Venue: \[Insert venue\]

---

# **6\. Privacy Review**

## **Eyebrow**

PRIVACY

## **Headline**

Privacy language must match the actual website setup.

## **Items to Confirm**

Analytics tool  
Cookie consent tool if used  
Form provider  
CRM or inbox tool  
Email marketing tool  
Scheduling tool  
Hosting provider  
Security or spam prevention tool  
Data retention practice  
International data processing  
Privacy rights language  
Service provider disclosures  
Whether marketing or retargeting cookies are used  
Whether newsletter signup is live  
Whether partnership or briefing forms are live

## **Rule**

Do not publish privacy language that names tools or practices that are not actually used.

Do not use advanced tracking or retargeting without updating the Privacy Notice and Cookie Policy.

---

# **7\. Cookie Review**

## **Eyebrow**

COOKIES

## **Headline**

Cookie language must match the final tracking setup.

## **Items to Confirm**

Essential cookies  
Analytics cookies  
Performance cookies  
Preference cookies  
Security cookies  
Marketing cookies if any  
Third-party cookies  
Cookie banner requirement  
Consent preference center if used  
Do Not Track language  
Browser privacy signal handling  
Cookie opt-out process

## **Launch Recommendation**

Use the simplest analytics setup possible at launch.

Avoid advertising, retargeting, or advanced behavioral tracking until the privacy and cookie language has been reviewed.

---

# **8\. Form and Intake Review**

## **Eyebrow**

FORMS

## **Headline**

Public forms should collect context, not sensitive files.

## **Forms to Review**

Book a C/Skills Review  
Contact  
Partnership Enquiry  
Briefing Request  
Newsletter / Updates Signup

## **Form Rules to Confirm**

No public file uploads at launch.  
No confidential-material upload.  
No legal-document upload.  
No credential collection.  
No private-link request.  
No sensitive personal data request.  
No information about minors requested.  
Clear confidentiality warning appears near forms.  
Submissions route to correct internal owner.  
Thank-you pages do not promise acceptance.  
Thank-you pages do not promise specific response timing unless operationally true.

## **Required Form Warning**

Do not send confidential source files, unreleased assets, private talent materials, contracts, legal documents, credentials, private links, or sensitive rights notes through this form.

---

# **9\. No Legal Advice Review**

## **Eyebrow**

NO LEGAL ADVICE

## **Headline**

Rights and governance pages need careful wording.

## **Allowed Language**

Rights review  
NILP® checks  
Consent review  
Talent usage controls  
Usage scope  
Review triggers  
Approval gates  
Escalation paths  
Protected records  
Legal boundary  
Client legal advisors  
Process design  
Creation Rights readiness

## **Avoided Language**

Legal advice  
Legal clearance by Creation Skills  
Rights clearance by Creation Skills  
Automatic rights approval  
Guaranteed compliance  
Risk-free media use  
Legal-safe by default  
No legal review needed  
AI-cleared rights  
Fully compliant outputs  
Insurance guarantee

## **Standard Boundary**

Creation Skills supports process design. It does not replace legal counsel. Legal decisions remain with the client and its advisors.

---

# **10\. Agent Claims Review**

## **Eyebrow**

PRODUCTION AGENTS

## **Headline**

Agent language should not overpromise automation or decision-making.

## **Allowed Language**

Agents can prepare.  
Agents can draft.  
Agents can summarize.  
Agents can flag.  
Agents can route.  
Agents can check.  
Agents can organize.  
Agents can prepare review packages.  
Agents require human approval gates.

## **Avoided Language**

Agents approve final media.  
Agents clear rights.  
Agents replace legal review.  
Agents make legal determinations.  
Agents approve NILP® usage.  
Agents approve talent usage.  
Agents publish final assets.  
Agents guarantee compliance.  
Agents make final decisions.

## **Required Core Line**

The agent does the prep.  
The human makes the call.  
The system records both.

---

# **11\. Creation Rights Readiness Review**

## **Eyebrow**

PLATFORM READINESS

## **Headline**

Creation Skills should not claim to be the platform.

## **Correct Split**

### **Creation Skills**

Prepares the company flow, production paths, production agents, operating files, human approval gates, rights review points, protected records, and Creation Rights readiness.

### **Creation Rights**

Governs rights, NILP®, consent, talent use, metadata, approvals, audit, payouts, and compliance-sensitive media records.

### **CreativeIP.org**

Supports education, certification, glossary, reference materials, professional development, and shared literacy.

## **Review Rule**

Creation Skills can prepare.

Creation Rights governs.

CreativeIP.org educates.

Do not blur the roles.

---

# **12\. Insurance, Risk, and Compliance Review**

## **Eyebrow**

RISK LANGUAGE

## **Headline**

Avoid overclaiming risk reduction.

## **Allowed Language**

Risk-aware  
Rights-aware  
Review points  
Approval gates  
Records  
Audit references  
Insurability support where appropriate  
Evidence support where appropriate  
Risk review support  
Process readiness

## **Avoided Language**

Guaranteed risk reduction  
Guaranteed coverage  
Fully insured  
Compliance guarantee  
Liability eliminated  
No infringement risk  
No legal exposure  
Insurance included by default

## **Counsel Note**

Any insurance, liability, compliance, or risk-transfer language should be reviewed separately before publication.

---

# **13\. Counsel Review Checklist**

## **Checklist**

Entity name confirmed.  
Registered address confirmed.  
Jurisdiction confirmed.  
Company number confirmed if applicable.  
Governing law confirmed.  
Venue confirmed.  
Trademark notices reviewed.  
NILP® mark language reviewed.  
Privacy Notice reviewed.  
Terms of Use reviewed.  
Cookie Policy reviewed.  
Legal Notice reviewed.  
No legal advice language confirmed.  
No client relationship language confirmed.  
No confidentiality overpromise.  
Public form warnings reviewed.  
No file upload at launch.  
No rights clearance claim.  
No compliance guarantee.  
No agent approval overclaim.  
No platform role confusion.  
No CIP role confusion.

---

# **14\. Legal Review Status Table**

## **Status Labels**

### **Not Started**

Page has not been sent to counsel.

### **In Review**

Page is with counsel.

### **Changes Needed**

Counsel returned edits or questions.

### **Approved for Launch**

Page can publish.

### **Approved With Placeholder**

Page can publish only with placeholder or limited language.

### **Hold**

Do not publish until legal or operational details are resolved.

## **Suggested Table Fields**

Page name  
Page type  
Review owner  
Legal reviewer  
Status  
Key issues  
Date sent  
Date returned  
Launch decision  
Notes

---

# **15\. Final Legal Handoff Note**

## **Headline**

Legal review protects the launch.

## **Body**

Creation Skills should launch with clear boundaries.

The offer is strong when the language is disciplined:

Creation Skills prepares the company.  
CreativeIP.org educates selected leaders.  
Creation Rights governs the protected layer.

The legal review should protect that split and keep all public claims accurate, cautious, and publishable.

# Launch Readiness, Staging, and Deployment Plan

# **Creation Skills Internal Page: Launch Readiness, Staging, and Deployment Plan**

## **Page Identity**

**Creation Skills**  
**C/Skills.**  
**A Creation Rights Division**

## **Document Type**

Internal launch operations document

## **Public Status**

Not a public page

## **Purpose**

This page defines the launch process for the Creation Skills website.

The goal is to move from master document to staging to public launch without exposing unfinished pages, legal placeholders, broken forms, internal planning notes, or incomplete routes.

The launch should feel premium, controlled, and ready for serious enterprise visitors.

---

# **1\. Launch Principle**

## **Headline**

Launch only the controlled version.

## **Body**

Creation Skills has a large content library, but the public site should launch with the focused launch set.

The master document should remain complete.

The first public website should remain selective.

The build should not expose phase two pages, audience detail pages, internal planning documents, or legal placeholders that have not been approved.

## **Core Rule**

The launch site should include only approved public pages, required utility pages, working forms, and safe confirmation routes.

---

# **2\. Launch Environments**

## **Eyebrow**

ENVIRONMENTS

## **Headline**

Use staging before public launch.

## **Recommended Environments**

### **Local Development**

Used for build work, component creation, page assembly, styling, routing, and early QA.

### **Staging**

Used for full content review, design review, legal review, form testing, analytics testing, mobile QA, and stakeholder approval.

### **Production**

Used for the public Creation Skills website after final approval.

## **Environment Rule**

No page should move to production before it has passed staging review.

---

# **3\. Staging Scope**

## **Eyebrow**

STAGING

## **Headline**

Staging should include the full launch set.

## **Pages to Stage**

Home  
What We Do  
Engagements  
C/Skills Review  
Operating Model Design  
Agent Operating Blueprint  
Governance Blueprint  
Pilot Transformation  
Implementation Office  
Who We Help  
Creation Rights Readiness  
About  
Book a C/Skills Review  
Contact  
Privacy Notice  
Terms of Use  
Cookie Policy  
Accessibility Statement  
Legal Notice / Company Information  
404 / Page Not Found  
C/Skills Review Confirmation  
Contact Confirmation

## **Conditional Staging Pages**

Partnership Enquiry Confirmation  
Briefing Request Confirmation  
Newsletter / Updates Confirmation

## **Optional Staging Pages**

Resources Index  
Why Production Agents Need Owners  
From Loose Prompts to Operating Files  
The Human Approval Gate  
Why Rights Review Belongs Earlier  
Creation Rights Readiness Resource Article

## **Staging Rule**

Optional pages should be staged only if they are intended for launch or near-launch review.

---

# **4\. Pages That Must Stay Hidden**

## **Eyebrow**

HIDDEN CONTENT

## **Headline**

Do not expose internal or staged pages.

## **Internal Pages Not for Public Launch**

Sitemap / Index Plan  
Final Launch Sitemap  
Final Menu Structure  
Final Build Prompt for Gemini / Claude Code  
Page Cutdown Plan  
Master Document Assembly Guide  
Master Page Status Index  
Final QA Checklist  
Content Freeze and Build Handoff  
Future Publishing Queue  
SEO Metadata and URL Map  
Form Routing and Intake Plan  
Component and Template Map  
Visual System and Art Direction  
Copy Style Guide and Messaging Rules  
Build File Structure and Route Map  
Analytics and Measurement Plan  
Legal Review Tracker and Counsel Handoff  
Launch Readiness, Staging, and Deployment Plan

## **Hidden Page Rule**

Internal pages should remain in the master document only.

They should not be built as public routes.

They should not be indexed.

They should not appear in navigation, footer, sitemap, or search.

---

# **5\. Pre-Staging Checklist**

## **Eyebrow**

PRE-STAGING

## **Headline**

Before staging, confirm the launch set.

## **Checklist**

Launch sitemap is approved.  
Menu structure is approved.  
Page cutdown plan is approved.  
Core page content is complete.  
Utility page content is complete.  
Legal-review pages are marked.  
SEO metadata exists for launch pages.  
Forms are defined.  
Confirmation routes are defined.  
No public file upload is included.  
Brand lockup is final.  
Visual system is approved.  
Copy style guide is approved.  
Internal pages are marked internal only.

---

# **6\. Staging Review Checklist**

## **Eyebrow**

STAGING REVIEW

## **Headline**

Use staging to review the real site, not just the content.

## **Review Areas**

### **Brand Review**

C/Skills. is correct.  
A Creation Rights Division is correct.  
NILP® mark is correct.  
Creation Skills, Creation Rights, and CreativeIP.org roles are clear.

### **Content Review**

No lorem ipsum.  
No placeholder nonsense text.  
No Gemini artifacts.  
No internal planning notes.  
No accidental draft comments.  
No “AI era.”  
No “diagnostic.”  
No generic AI consulting tone.

### **Design Review**

Visual system feels premium.  
Lime identity is clear.  
Spacing is strong.  
Mobile layout works.  
Forms feel calm and serious.  
CTAs are clear.

### **Legal Review**

Privacy Notice reviewed.  
Terms of Use reviewed.  
Cookie Policy reviewed.  
Legal Notice reviewed.  
Legal-sensitive pages approved or held.  
No legal advice claims.  
No rights clearance claims.  
No compliance guarantees.

### **Form Review**

Forms submit correctly.  
Confirmation pages load.  
Field labels are clear.  
Error messages are readable.  
Confidentiality warning appears.  
No file upload is active.

### **SEO Review**

Metadata appears.  
URLs are clean.  
No duplicate titles.  
Thank-you pages are noindex.  
Internal pages are not indexed.  
Sitemap includes only approved public pages.

### **Technical Review**

Header works.  
Footer works.  
Mobile menu works.  
404 works.  
No broken links.  
No broken images.  
No console errors.  
Site loads quickly.

---

# **7\. Launch Approval Owners**

## **Eyebrow**

OWNERSHIP

## **Headline**

Assign approval before production launch.

## **Required Owners**

Content owner  
Design owner  
Development owner  
Legal reviewer  
SEO reviewer  
Accessibility reviewer  
Form routing owner  
Analytics owner  
Final launch approver

## **Approval Rule**

No production launch without named approval from each required owner.

---

# **8\. Form Launch Requirements**

## **Eyebrow**

FORMS

## **Headline**

Forms must be safe before launch.

## **Required Form Rules**

No public file upload.  
No confidential-material upload.  
No credential collection.  
No legal-document collection.  
No private-link request.  
No sensitive personal data request.  
No information about minors requested.  
Clear confidentiality warning appears near forms.  
Submissions route to the correct destination.  
Thank-you pages do not promise acceptance.  
Thank-you pages do not promise response timing unless confirmed.

## **Required Form Warning**

Do not send confidential source files, unreleased assets, private talent materials, contracts, legal documents, credentials, private links, or sensitive rights notes through this form.

---

# **9\. Analytics Launch Requirements**

## **Eyebrow**

ANALYTICS

## **Headline**

Analytics should be simple and privacy-aware.

## **Required Checks**

Analytics tool is selected.  
Privacy Notice matches analytics setup.  
Cookie Policy matches cookie setup.  
Consent approach is confirmed if needed.  
Form events are defined.  
CTA events are defined.  
Thank-you page views are tracked.  
Internal pages are excluded.  
No confidential form message content is tracked.  
Test submissions are excluded where possible.

## **Launch Events**

cta\_book\_review\_click  
book\_review\_form\_view  
book\_review\_form\_start  
book\_review\_form\_submit  
contact\_form\_submit  
thank\_you\_review\_view  
thank\_you\_contact\_view

---

# **10\. SEO and Indexing Requirements**

## **Eyebrow**

INDEXING

## **Headline**

Only approved public pages should be indexed.

## **Index at Launch**

Home  
What We Do  
Engagements  
C/Skills Review  
Operating Model Design  
Agent Operating Blueprint  
Governance Blueprint  
Pilot Transformation  
Implementation Office  
Who We Help  
Creation Rights Readiness  
About  
Book a C/Skills Review  
Contact  
Approved utility pages if appropriate

## **Noindex at Launch**

Thank-you pages  
Internal planning pages  
Staged pages  
Draft pages  
Preview routes  
Legal pages not yet approved  
Optional resources not approved for launch

## **Sitemap Rule**

The public sitemap should include only approved public pages.

---

# **11\. Deployment Checklist**

## **Eyebrow**

DEPLOYMENT

## **Headline**

Final checks before production.

## **Checklist**

Production domain confirmed.  
SSL certificate active.  
Environment variables set.  
Form routing tested.  
Analytics tested.  
Robots rules checked.  
Sitemap checked.  
Canonical URLs checked.  
404 page tested.  
Legal footer links tested.  
Mobile navigation tested.  
Performance checked.  
Accessibility quick pass completed.  
Final content approval received.  
Final legal approval received where needed.  
Rollback plan confirmed.

---

# **12\. Rollback Plan**

## **Eyebrow**

ROLLBACK

## **Headline**

Have a simple rollback path.

## **Body**

If the production launch has a major issue, the team should know how to return to the prior stable version.

## **Rollback Triggers**

Forms are not working.  
Legal pages are missing.  
Internal pages are exposed.  
Wrong brand lockup is live.  
Sensitive placeholder content is visible.  
Navigation is broken.  
Production domain is misconfigured.  
Analytics or cookie setup is incorrect.  
Major mobile issue appears.  
Security or privacy issue appears.

## **Rollback Actions**

Pause promotion.  
Revert to prior stable deployment.  
Disable broken form if needed.  
Remove exposed internal routes.  
Fix issue in staging.  
Re-test affected pages.  
Re-deploy after approval.

---

# **13\. Launch Day Checklist**

## **Eyebrow**

LAUNCH DAY

## **Headline**

Check the public site after deployment.

## **Checklist**

Homepage loads.  
Navigation works.  
Footer works.  
Book Review page loads.  
Contact page loads.  
Forms submit.  
Thank-you pages load.  
Legal pages load.  
404 works.  
Mobile menu works.  
SEO metadata appears.  
No internal pages are visible.  
No draft pages are indexed.  
Analytics records test event.  
Site speed is acceptable.  
Stakeholders can access the live URL.

---

# **14\. Post-Launch Review**

## **Eyebrow**

POST-LAUNCH

## **Headline**

Review the site after real use begins.

## **Week One Review**

Check form submissions.  
Check broken links.  
Check 404 hits.  
Check top landing pages.  
Check CTA clicks.  
Check mobile issues.  
Check any user feedback.

## **Month One Review**

Review lead quality.  
Review page performance.  
Review form completion.  
Review Creation Rights readiness interest.  
Review whether Resources should launch.  
Review phase two publishing priority.

## **Rule**

Use post-launch data to improve clarity, not to chase random traffic.

---

# **15\. Final Launch Approval Line**

## **Headline**

Ready to launch when the system is controlled.

## **Body**

The Creation Skills website is ready for production only when:

The launch set is frozen.  
The menu is simple.  
The content is complete.  
The forms are safe.  
The legal pages are reviewed or clearly held.  
The internal pages are hidden.  
The brand is consistent.  
The site feels premium.  
The main CTA is clear.

## **Core Launch Path**

Visitor understands the offer.  
Visitor sees the C/Skills Review path.  
Visitor submits one focused production path for review.

---

# **Final Note**

The launch should reflect the same standard Creation Skills brings to client work:

The system should be clear before it scales.

# Content Maintenance and Governance Plan

# **Creation Skills Internal Page: Content Maintenance and Governance Plan**

## **Page Identity**

**Creation Skills**  
**C/Skills.**  
**A Creation Rights Division**

## **Document Type**

Internal content governance document

## **Public Status**

Not a public page

## **Purpose**

This page defines how Creation Skills website content should be maintained after launch.

The goal is to keep the site accurate, premium, legally cautious, and aligned with the Creation Rights ecosystem as new pages, resources, audience pages, concept pages, and form paths are added.

Creation Skills has a deep content library. That library should be managed carefully so the public site does not drift into generic AI consulting, public course language, legal advice, platform claims, or cluttered navigation.

---

# **1\. Governance Principle**

## **Headline**

The site should stay controlled as it grows.

## **Body**

Creation Skills should grow through deliberate publishing, not page sprawl.

Every new page should support the core offer:

Creation Skills helps companies prepare the company flow behind modern media creation through people, production agents, operating files, human approval gates, rights review, protected records, and Creation Rights readiness.

## **Core Rule**

Do not publish a page just because it exists in the master document.

Publish a page only when it supports the strategy, has a clear CTA, passes review, and fits the current site structure.

---

# **2\. Source of Truth**

## **Eyebrow**

SOURCE OF TRUTH

## **Headline**

The master document controls the content library.

## **Body**

The Creation Skills master document is the source of truth for approved page content, internal planning pages, publishing queues, metadata, legal-review notes, and build prompts.

The website should be built from the approved launch set, not from every page in the master document.

## **Source Hierarchy**

### **1\. Master Document**

Holds the complete content library.

### **2\. Launch Sitemap**

Controls what is built and published first.

### **3\. Menu Structure**

Controls what appears in navigation.

### **4\. Page Status Index**

Controls page status, URL, review needs, and publishing readiness.

### **5\. Build File Structure and Route Map**

Controls implementation.

### **6\. QA Checklist**

Controls final review before launch or update.

## **Rule**

If the master document and the live site disagree, update the master document first, then update the site.

---

# **3\. Page Ownership**

## **Eyebrow**

OWNERSHIP

## **Headline**

Every page should have an owner.

## **Body**

Each page should have a named owner responsible for accuracy, updates, review status, and publishing decisions.

## **Ownership Roles**

### **Content Owner**

Responsible for page copy, positioning, tone, and clarity.

### **Design Owner**

Responsible for page layout, visual consistency, and brand feel.

### **Development Owner**

Responsible for route, component use, metadata, performance, and technical implementation.

### **Legal Reviewer**

Responsible for legal-sensitive content, privacy, terms, cookies, notices, rights language, NILP® language, and no-legal-advice boundaries.

### **SEO Reviewer**

Responsible for metadata, URL, indexing, internal links, and search intent.

### **Final Approver**

Responsible for deciding whether the page can publish.

## **Rule**

No new public page should publish without a content owner and final approver.

---

# **4\. Page Status Management**

## **Eyebrow**

STATUS

## **Headline**

Each page needs a current status.

## **Status Labels**

### **Draft**

Content exists but is not ready for review.

### **Content Review**

Page is being checked for clarity, positioning, and tone.

### **Legal Review**

Page is with counsel or needs legal-sensitive review.

### **Design Ready**

Copy is approved and ready for design.

### **Build Ready**

Copy and design direction are approved for implementation.

### **Staged**

Page is built on staging but not public.

### **Approved for Launch**

Page is ready to publish.

### **Published**

Page is live.

### **Hold**

Page should not publish yet.

### **Retired**

Page has been removed from the public site but preserved in the master document.

## **Rule**

A page should not move from Draft to Published without passing the required review steps.

---

# **5\. Change Control**

## **Eyebrow**

CHANGE CONTROL

## **Headline**

Important content changes should be reviewed before they go live.

## **Body**

Creation Skills content touches consulting, rights review, NILP®, production agents, protected records, platform readiness, and legal-sensitive boundaries.

Small copy fixes can move quickly.

Substantive changes should be reviewed.

## **Minor Changes**

Typos  
Formatting fixes  
Broken link fixes  
CTA label consistency  
Small clarity edits  
SEO description refinements

## **Major Changes**

New page launch  
New service description  
New legal-sensitive claim  
New form field  
New data collection method  
New analytics or tracking tool  
New partnership language  
New Creation Rights platform claim  
New CIP certification or education language  
New rights, NILP®, consent, or compliance language  
New pricing or commercial terms if added later

## **Rule**

Major changes require content review and, where relevant, legal review before publication.

---

# **6\. Copy Consistency Review**

## **Eyebrow**

COPY

## **Headline**

All updates must follow the Creation Skills language rules.

## **Required Language**

Creation Skills  
C/Skills.  
A Creation Rights Division  
Creation Rights  
CreativeIP.org  
NILP®  
Company flow  
Production path  
Production agents  
Company-owned production agents  
Operating files  
Human approval gates  
Rights review  
Protected records  
Creation Rights readiness

## **Avoided Language**

AI era  
Diagnostic  
Generic AI training  
Tool consulting  
Course  
Academy  
Automation agency  
Bot catalog  
Workflow as the main repeated word  
Legal clearance by Creation Skills  
Guaranteed compliance  
Autonomous approval  
Risk-free media use

## **Core Line**

The agent does the prep.  
The human makes the call.  
The system records both.

## **Rule**

Any new copy should be checked against the Copy Style Guide and Messaging Rules before publication.

---

# **7\. Ecosystem Boundary Review**

## **Eyebrow**

ECOSYSTEM BOUNDARIES

## **Headline**

Do not blur Creation Skills, Creation Rights, and CreativeIP.org.

## **Correct Split**

### **Creation Skills**

The consulting and transformation layer.

Prepares the company flow, production paths, production agents, operating files, human approval gates, rights review points, protected records, and Creation Rights readiness.

### **Creation Rights**

The platform layer.

Governs rights, NILP®, consent, talent use, metadata, approvals, audit, payouts, and compliance-sensitive media records.

### **CreativeIP.org**

The education and standards layer.

Supports public education, professional development, certification, glossary, reference materials, and shared literacy around creative intellectual property.

## **Required Line**

Creation Skills prepares the company.  
CreativeIP.org educates selected leaders.  
Creation Rights governs the protected layer.

## **Rule**

Every page that mentions the ecosystem should preserve this split.

---

# **8\. Legal-Sensitive Content Review**

## **Eyebrow**

LEGAL-SENSITIVE CONTENT

## **Headline**

Rights, NILP®, consent, records, privacy, and claims language need extra care.

## **Pages Requiring Care**

Privacy Notice  
Terms of Use  
Cookie Policy  
Legal Notice / Company Information  
Confidentiality and Client Materials  
Rights Review and NILP® Checks  
Protected Records and Metadata  
Asset Reuse and Usage Controls  
Responsible Agent Use  
Legal, Rights, and Risk Teams  
Why Rights Review Belongs Earlier

## **Review Questions**

Does the page imply Creation Skills provides legal advice?

Does the page imply Creation Skills clears rights?

Does the page imply agents make legal determinations?

Does the page imply guaranteed compliance?

Does the page overpromise confidentiality before an agreement?

Does the page collect sensitive information through public forms?

Does the page confuse consulting with platform governance?

Does the page need counsel review before publishing?

## **Required Boundary**

Creation Skills supports process design. It does not replace legal counsel. Legal decisions remain with the client and its advisors.

---

# **9\. Form Governance**

## **Eyebrow**

FORMS

## **Headline**

Forms should stay safe as the site grows.

## **Body**

Forms are one of the highest-risk parts of the website because users may try to submit sensitive materials.

## **Form Rules**

No public file uploads unless specifically approved.

No confidential material collection through public forms.

No credentials or access tokens.

No legal document uploads.

No private talent material uploads.

No sensitive rights-note uploads.

No unnecessary sensitive personal data.

No information about minors requested.

## **Required Warning**

Do not send confidential source files, unreleased assets, private talent materials, contracts, legal documents, credentials, private links, or sensitive rights notes through this form.

## **Rule**

Any new form field should be reviewed before launch.

---

# **10\. Navigation Governance**

## **Eyebrow**

NAVIGATION

## **Headline**

The navigation should remain smaller than the content library.

## **Body**

As more pages are published, the top navigation should stay simple.

The site can have depth without exposing every page in the header.

## **Top Navigation**

C/Skills.  
What We Do  
Engagements  
Who We Help  
Creation Rights Readiness  
About  
Book a C/Skills Review

## **Footer Can Expand**

The footer can carry more links across:

Creation Skills  
Engagements  
Ecosystem  
Resources  
Legal

## **Rule**

Do not add phase two, phase three, or resource pages to the top navigation unless they support a major buyer path.

---

# **11\. Publishing Cadence**

## **Eyebrow**

PUBLISHING CADENCE

## **Headline**

Publish in controlled batches.

## **Body**

Publishing too many pages at once can make the site harder to review, harder to QA, and less focused.

## **Recommended Cadence**

One to three pages per release batch.

Each batch should have:

Content review  
SEO review  
Internal links  
CTA check  
Legal review if needed  
Design QA  
Staging QA  
Launch decision

## **Rule**

Do not publish a batch unless every page in the batch has a clear purpose and owner.

---

# **12\. Quarterly Content Review**

## **Eyebrow**

REVIEW CADENCE

## **Headline**

Review the site regularly after launch.

## **Monthly Review**

Check form submissions.  
Check lead quality.  
Check top pages.  
Check broken links.  
Check 404s.  
Check CTA performance.  
Check page feedback.  
Check if any copy is confusing visitors.

## **Quarterly Review**

Review all public pages.  
Confirm legal pages are current.  
Confirm privacy and cookie tools match policies.  
Review analytics setup.  
Review top navigation.  
Review footer links.  
Review resource performance.  
Update phase two and phase three publishing priorities.  
Retire or revise weak pages.

## **Annual Review**

Review the whole master document.  
Review ecosystem positioning.  
Review legal disclaimers.  
Review trademark notices.  
Review service descriptions.  
Review forms and data collection.  
Review page status index.  
Review build prompts and design direction.

---

# **13\. Content Retirement**

## **Eyebrow**

RETIREMENT

## **Headline**

Some pages may need to be removed or held.

## **Reasons to Retire or Hold a Page**

The service is no longer offered.

The page creates confusion.

The page overlaps too much with another page.

The legal risk is too high.

The page is outdated.

The page receives the wrong kind of enquiries.

The page makes Creation Skills sound generic.

The page blurs Creation Skills with Creation Rights or CreativeIP.org.

## **Retirement Process**

Mark page as Retired in the master document.

Remove from navigation and sitemap.

Redirect if the page was public and indexed.

Preserve the original copy in the master document.

Update internal links.

Update SEO map.

---

# **14\. Version Control**

## **Eyebrow**

VERSION CONTROL

## **Headline**

Track major content changes.

## **Suggested Version Notes**

Page name  
Date changed  
Change owner  
Reason for change  
Summary of changes  
Legal review required  
SEO impact  
Published or staged  
Approver

## **Rule**

Major page changes should be documented in the master document or project tracker.

---

# **15\. Governance QA Checklist**

## **Checklist**

Page has a current status.  
Page has an owner.  
Page has a clear CTA.  
Page has SEO metadata.  
Page uses correct brand language.  
Page does not use avoided language.  
Page preserves the ecosystem split.  
Page has legal review if needed.  
Page has safe form language if relevant.  
Page has internal links.  
Page is in the correct navigation or footer location.  
Page is staged before publishing.  
Page is not indexed before approval.  
Page is added to the page status index.

---

# **Final Note**

Creation Skills should grow like a controlled system.

The master document can stay deep.  
The public site should stay clear.  
Every new page should make the offer sharper.
